title: Prevent Threads from Starting in Constructors
description: Starting threads in a constructor can potentially lead to issues in the case of class extension. The superclass' constructor will start the thread before the child class has been fully initialized. To avoid such complications, avoid invoking Thread.start() in constructors.
recommendation: Refrain from starting threads in constructors, especially in non-final classes. Consider initializing and starting threads in a separate method outside the constructor.
impact: If not followed, this could lead to unanticipated behavior in subclasses and potential vulnerabilities in your Java code.

checks.message: Refrain from starting threads in constructors, especially in non-final classes. Consider initializing and starting threads in a separate method outside the constructor.
